name: Sno Falls
description_short: Sno Falls Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution serving Washington since 1957, offering savings, checking, loans, and investment products with competitive rates and local decision-making.
description_long:
Sno Falls Credit Union has been operating as a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union in Washington state since 1957. The institution positions itself around three core principles: competitive rates, fast service, and local decision-making. With two physical locations in the Snoqualmie Valley area, the credit union maintains a community-focused approach to banking and lending.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including deposit accounts (savings, checking, money market, CDs), credit products (auto loans, personal loans, home loans, home equity lines of credit, business loans), credit cards with rewards programs, and investment products (traditional and Roth IRAs, Coverdell ESAs). They feature specialized loan programs such as 90-day skip-pay auto loans (as low as 4.74% APR), youth savings and checking accounts with high APY rates (up to 4.53%), and Swipe & Save automated savings features. Business services include business checking, savings, money market accounts, and an SBA loan program.

Distinguishing features include access to 30,000+ ATMs through their network, Zelle money transfer integration, online and mobile banking platforms, a Business Partnership Program that provides community visibility for local merchants, and member-only benefits. They offer specialized loan categories including tiny home loans, RV loans, motorcycle loans, and boat loans—demonstrating niche market focus. Their "Meet or Beat" rate promise and loan calculator tools add transparency to their lending process.

As a credit union, Sno Falls operates under NCUA insurance rather than FDIC insurance, and membership eligibility appears limited to the local Snoqualmie Valley service area. While they advertise competitive rates and fast auto loan pre-approval processes, the website does not provide detailed information about membership requirements, credit score minimums, or comprehensive rate comparisons. The institution serves both individual and business members with emphasis on personal banking and auto lending.
services: Member savings accounts with Swipe & Save automation
Youth Savings and Youth Rewards Checking (ages 14-17)
Rewards Checking with Shopspot Rewards Program
MasterCard Platinum Cash Rewards and standard MasterCard
Auto loans (new, used, first-time buyers, sport vehicles, tiny homes, RVs, motorcycles, boats)
Business checking, savings, and money market accounts
Traditional, Roth, and Coverdell ESA IRAs
pros: Access to 30,000+ ATM network for members
Youth Rewards Checking with 4.53% APY on balances up to $5,000 for ages 14-17
Swipe & Save feature earning 5.12% APY on balances up to $2,500
90-day skip-pay auto loans with rates starting at 4.74% APR
No minimum balance requirements and no monthly service fees on checking accounts
SBA loan program specifically designed for small businesses
Local decision-making and two physical branch locations in service area
Specialized loan products including tiny homes, RVs, motorcycles, and boats
cons: Limited service area—appears to serve only Snoqualmie Valley/Washington region, restricting membership eligibility
Website does not clearly state membership requirements or eligibility criteria
Auto loan APR range is wide (4.74%-15.49% for new, 5.24%-15.24% for used), suggesting approval-dependent pricing with potential for high rates
Personal/lifestyle loan rates reach 22.49% APR, which is on the higher end for credit unions
Only two physical branch locations limits accessibility for members outside immediate area
best_for: Washington residents in the Snoqualmie Valley area seeking local banking with community focus
Auto buyers wanting quick pre-approval and 90-day skip-pay options
Youth ages 14-17 whose parents want high-yield savings accounts with no fees
Small business owners interested in SBA loans and community partnership visibility
diagnosis: Sno Falls Credit Union is best for Washington residents in the Snoqualmie Valley area who prioritize local banking relationships, competitive rates on auto loans, and member-focused benefits. The main caveat is limited geographic service area and restricted membership eligibility tied to the local community—this is not an option for individuals outside the Snoqualmie Valley region.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Sno Falls — Credit Union in North Bend, WA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Sno Falls — NCUA-insured credit union in North Bend, WA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
